description_long: This is a traditional East Asian pagoda structure set within a landscaped area featuring symmetrical rows of pink-blossomed cherry trees. The pagoda has two prominent stories, each with a wide, gently sloping tiled roof and upward-curving eaves, supported by red columns with gold accents. The entrance is marked by a paved stone walkway leading up to the open front, which is flanked by the cherry trees. The rooflines are layered, creating a visually striking silhouette, but there is no visible ornamental finial at the apex. The structure is primarily constructed from stone and wood, with the tiled roof and wooden columns providing both support and aesthetic detail. The surrounding area is covered in grass and stone paving, enhancing the serene, park-like setting.
description: A two-story East Asian pagoda with upward-curving tiled roofs, red columns, and surrounded by cherry blossom trees on a stone-paved landscape.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, red columns, and symmetrical cherry trees flanking the stone walkway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered roof structure and the rows of cherry trees extending along the length of the pagoda.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the tiled roofs and cherry trees visible, but no entrance details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the multi-tiered roof and the dense arrangement of cherry trees.
